ddls:
CREATE TABLE "categories" (
  "category_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each category */,
  "category_name" TEXT /* Name of the category (e.g., Biochemical Tests) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the category */,
  "category_type" TEXT /* Type of category (e.g., biochemical, hematological) */,
  "sub_category" TEXT /* Sub-category of the test */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "patients" (
  "patient_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each patient */,
  "date_of_birth" TEXT /* Date of birth of the patient */,
  "age" INTEGER /* Age of the patient */,
  "sex" INTEGER /* Sex of the patient (1: Male, 2: Female) */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the patient */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email of the patient */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the patient */,
  "medical_history" TEXT /* Medical history of the patient */,
  "current_medication" TEXT /* Current medication of the patient */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("patient_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "clusters" (
  "cluster_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each cluster */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the criteria for the cluster */,
  "cluster_criteria" TEXT /* Specific criteria for clustering test results */,
  "cluster_size" INTEGER /* Number of test results in the cluster */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("cluster_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "test_results" (
  "patient_id" INTEGER,
  "test_name" TEXT,
  "test_value" INTEGER,
  FOREIGN KEY ("patient_id") REFERENCES "patients" ("patient_id")
)
column_names: Name of the category (e.g., Biochemical Tests)
Date of birth of the patient
Sex of the patient (1: Male, 2: Female)
Description of the criteria for the cluster
Specific criteria for clustering test results
Number of test results in the cluster
